title: DevLocators
description: Dev-team-supplied (or external-LLM-produced) mapping of selectors the JIT runtime consults BEFORE the LLM resolver. Two match modes: Tier 1a (exact key) — JSON keys equal qtea's generated constant names (HITL-replay path). Tier 1b (intent pool) — entries carry an `intent` description and the runtime fuzzy-matches `tbd(...)` intents against the pool, so frontend devs can use arbitrary key names. Discovery precedence: --dev-locators flag > QTEA_DEV_LOCATORS env > `<workspace>/locator-cache/dev-locators.json` default. Selectors are verified at first use; mismatches fall through to the resolver.

version.description: Schema version, e.g. "1.0". Allows the loader to handle future shape changes.

source.description: Free-text identifier of who produced this file (e.g. "dev-team", "external-llm", "parent-qtea", "manual"). For traceability in the Step 10 report.

locators.description: Mapping of constant name (the same name codegen used in the locators module, e.g. LOGIN_BUTTON) to its dev-supplied selector + metadata.

selector.description: The selector to use. XPath is rejected at load time (the loader filters out entries whose selector starts with `//` or `xpath=`).

payload.description: Structured locator payload for role/text/label/placeholder/test_id strategies. When set, the runtime uses page.get_by_role(...) etc. at action time instead of page.locator(string); TBD promotion writes a role_locator(...) call into source. Optional — when null, the runtime falls back to the `selector` string. Validated by qtea.jit_resolver.validate_selector_payload at load time.

name.description: Optional accessible name for role kind.

text.description: Required when kind in {text, label, placeholder}.

intent.description: Human description of what this locator targets, e.g. 'submits the login form'. When present, this entry participates in Tier 1b intent-pool matching against `tbd(...)` strings (frontend-dev workflow). Without `intent`, the entry is only reachable via Tier 1a exact-key match (HITL-replay). Write the way you'd describe the element to a new teammate, not as a CSS class label.

page_url.description: URL or path of the page where this element lives. Optional but recommended for Tier 1b in multi-page apps with structurally similar elements (e.g. a Submit button on every form): when set and the current page differs, the entry's pool-match score is reduced by QTEA_DEV_POOL_PAGE_PENALTY (default 0.15, soft penalty — not a hard filter).
